HMO Manager Job at Syx-Brij Nigeria Limited




        
    

        
        Syx-Brij Nigeria Limited - Our client, Paz Diagnostics, is recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: HMO Manager
Job Location: Ikorodu, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary


	Paz Diagnostics is seeking an experienced, proactive, and results-driven HMO Manager to oversee and grow our relationships with Health Maintenance Organizations (HMOs), corporate organizations, and healthcare partners.
	The ideal candidate will ensure seamless coordination between HMOs and our diagnostic services while driving business growth and maintaining excellent client satisfaction.


Key Responsibilities
     

            
    




	Develop and maintain strong relationships with HMOs, insurance providers, and corporate clients.
	Negotiate and manage HMO contracts, pricing, and service agreements.
	Coordinate HMO patient authorization, billing, claims processing, and reconciliation.
	Ensure timely submission and follow-up of HMO claims to minimize payment delays.
	Resolve HMO-related complaints and service issues promptly.
	Identify and pursue new HMO partnerships and business opportunities.
	Monitor HMO performance, utilization trends, and revenue generation.
	Collaborate with internal departments to ensure efficient service delivery.
	Prepare periodic reports on HMO activities, revenue, outstanding claims, and key performance indicators.
	Ensure compliance with all contractual obligations and healthcare regulations.


Requirements


	Bachelor's Degree in Business Administration, Healthcare Management, Marketing, Public Health, or a related field.
	2–5 years of experience in HMO management, healthcare marketing, or business development within the healthcare industry.
	Strong understanding of HMO operations, claims management, and healthcare billing processes.
	Excellent negotiation, communication, and relationship management skills.
	Strong analytical and problem-solving abilities.
	Proficiency in Microsoft Office (Excel, Word, PowerPoint) and healthcare management software is an advantage.
	Ability to work independently and achieve business growth targets.


What We Offer


	Salary: N150,000 - N200,000 per month take-home salary and performance-based incentives.
	Professional growth and career development opportunities.
	Supportive and collaborative work environment.
	Opportunity to contribute to improving healthcare delivery.

Bidding and Tendering Engineer Job at Ellasot Consulting




        
    

        
        Ellasot Consulting - Our client, a reputable company, is recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Bidding and Tendering Engineer 

Job Location: Port Harcourt, Rivers
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Description
     

            
    




	Our client is seeking a competent and detail-oriented Bidding and Tendering Engineerin the oil and gas sector, responsible for identifying business opportunities, preparing competitive technical and commercial proposals, coordinating bid submissions, and ensuring compliance with client and regulatory requirements.
	The ideal candidate will possess a strong background in chemical engineering, proven sales experience, and a deep understanding of procurement processes in the upstream oil and gas industry.
	The ideal candidate will be responsible for managing the end-to-end tendering process for oil and gas projects.
	This includes reviewing tender documents, preparing technical and commercial proposals, coordinating with multidisciplinary teams, estimating project costs, assessing risks, and submitting competitive bids that align with company objectives and client requirements


Key Responsibilities


	Monitor tender portals, client websites, and procurement platforms for new business opportunities.
	Review Invitations to Tender (ITT), Requests for Proposal (RFP), Requests for Quotation (RFQ), and pre-qualification documents.
	Analyze tender specifications, scope of work, drawings, and contractual requirements.
	Coordinate with engineering, procurement, construction, operations, HSE, QA/QC, finance, and supply chain teams to prepare comprehensive bids.
	Prepare technical proposals in accordance with client specifications and industry standards.
	Develop commercial proposals including pricing, cost estimates, schedules, and payment terms.
	Perform quantity take-offs and cost estimation for mechanical, electrical, instrumentation, piping, civil, and maintenance projects.
	Obtain quotations from suppliers, subcontractors, and vendors.
	Evaluate vendor quotations and recommend the most competitive technical and commercial options.
	Conduct risk assessments and identify contractual, commercial, and technical risks associated with each tender.
	Prepare bid clarifications and respond to client technical queries during the tender stage.
	Ensure compliance with client requirements, company procedures, and applicable oil and gas industry standards.
	Coordinate bid review meetings and obtain management approvals before submission.
	Submit tenders within specified deadlines through electronic procurement portals or hard-copy submissions.
	Maintain an organized database of tender documents, bid history, and pricing information.
	Participate in post-tender negotiations, technical clarification meetings, and commercial discussions with clients.
	Support contract handover to project execution teams after award.
	Analyze unsuccessful bids and recommend improvements for future tenders.
	Maintain confidentiality of commercial and technical information throughout the bidding process.


Required Qualifications


	Bachelor's Degree in Mechanical Engineering, Electrical Engineering, Civil Engineering, Chemical Engineering, Petroleum Engineering, or a related engineering discipline.
	Professional certifications in Project Management, Cost Estimation, Contracts, or Procurement are an advantage.
	5–10 years of experience in bidding and tendering within the oil and gas industry.
	Experience with EPC, EPCIC, maintenance, shutdown, brownfield, or greenfield projects.
	Experience preparing technical and commercial proposals for upstream, midstream, or downstream oil and gas projects.
	Familiarity with international oil companies (IOCs), national oil companies (NOCs), and major EPC contractors.


Technical Skills:


	Tender preparation and bid management
	Technical proposal development
	Cost estimation and budgeting
	Contract review and interpretation
	Risk assessment
	Vendor and subcontractor evaluation
	Commercial analysis
	Quantity take-off
	Project scheduling
	Procurement processes
	Knowledge of engineering drawings and specifications
	Proficiency in Microsoft Office Suite (Excel, Word, PowerPoint)
	Experience with ERP systems such as SAP, Oracle, or Microsoft Dynamics
	Familiarity with estimating software and document management systems.


Note: Only candidates resident in, or able to relocate to, Port Harcourt will be considered.

Backend Developer (Java) Job at Tezza Business Solutions Limited




        
    

        
        Tezza Business Solutions Limited is a leading Software Quality Assurance and Testing multinational with a regional office in Lagos, Nigeria. We are a Software Quality Assurance and Testing Services provider. Our primary goal is to be the "go to" Quality Assurance & Software Testing company in East and West Africa while developing offshore resources to fill test automation positions (and other hard to find software testing capabilities) in the North America and European markets.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Backend Developer (Java)

Job Location: Lagos - Remote
Employment Type: Contract

About the Role
     

            
    




	We are seeking an experienced Backend Developer (Java) to design, build, and maintain scalable, high-performance backend systems.
	The ideal candidate has strong expertise in Java-based microservices architecture, is comfortable with cloud-native applications, and can work collaboratively in an agile environment to deliver reliable and efficient software solutions.


Key Responsibilities


	Design, develop, and maintain backend services and APIs using Java and related frameworks (Spring Boot, Hibernate, etc.).
	Architect and implement scalable microservices that support business applications and integrations.
	Optimize system performance, ensuring high availability, scalability, and security.
	Collaborate with frontend developers, DevOps engineers, and product managers to deliver end-to-end solutions.
	Implement data storage solutions (SQL and NoSQL databases) and optimize database queries.
	Integrate external systems and third-party APIs securely and efficiently.
	Participate in code reviews, ensure adherence to best practices, and maintain high code quality.
	Troubleshoot production issues, perform root cause analysis, and implement robust fixes.
	Contribute to CI/CD pipelines and automated testing frameworks.
	Stay current with emerging technologies, frameworks, and industry trends.


Required Qualifications


	Bachelor’s Degree in Computer Science, Engineering, or a related field.
	7–10 years of professional backend development experience with Java.
	Strong hands-on experience with Spring Framework (Spring Boot, Spring Security, Spring Cloud).
	Proven experience building and deploying microservices architectures.
	Proficiency with RESTful API design and development.
	Experience with Relational (MySQL, PostgreSQL) and NoSQL (MongoDB, Redis) databases.
	Solid understanding of software design patterns, data structures, and algorithms.
	Experience with containerization and orchestration tools (Docker, Kubernetes).
	Familiarity with CI/CD tools (Jenkins, GitLab CI, CircleCI) and version control (Git).
	Strong debugging, performance tuning, and problem-solving skills.


Preferred Qualifications:


	Experience with cloud platforms such as AWS, Azure, or GCP.
	Knowledge of message brokers (Kafka, RabbitMQ, or ActiveMQ).
	Exposure to API gateways, authentication, and authorization protocols (OAuth2, JWT).
	Experience in test-driven development (TDD) and automated testing frameworks (JUnit, Mockito).
	Familiarity with DevOps principles and infrastructure-as-code tools (Terraform, Ansible).
	Contribution to open-source projects or technical communities.


Soft Skills:


	Excellent communication and collaboration skills.
	Strong analytical thinking and attention to detail.
	Ability to mentor junior engineers and review peer code.
	Self-driven, proactive, and adaptable to a fast-paced environment.

Corporate Sales Head Job at Ellasot Consulting




        
    

        
        Ellasot Consulting is an organizational development and Human Resource Management advisory firm that provides expert advice and support to organizations to improve workforce performance and align strategies with objectives.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Corporate Sales Head

Job Location: Port Harcourt, Rivers
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary 
     

            
    




	Ellasot Consulting is recruiting on behalf of a reputable company seeking a competent and detail-oriented Corporate Sales Head to lead business development and technical sales activities for our drilling, production, and completion chemical solutions within the Oil & Gas sector.
	The ideal candidate will possess a strong chemical background, proven sales experience, and a deep understanding of procurement processes in the upstream oil and gas industry.
	The ideal candidate will be responsible for identifying new business opportunities, generating leads, building strong client relationships, and increasing company revenue.
	The ideal candidate should have solid experience in sales and business development within the real estate industry and possess a strong understanding of the Nigerian property market.


Key Responsibilities


	Drive sales and business development activities for drilling, production, and completion chemicals within the Oil & Gas industry.
	Identify, develop, and convert new business opportunities to achieve revenue and profitability targets.
	Build and maintain strong relationships with operators, service companies, procurement teams, and key decision-makers.
	Manage the entire sales cycle from prospecting and lead generation through contract award and customer support.
	Prepare and submit technical and commercial proposals in response to client requirements and tenders.
	Coordinate vendor registration processes with oil and gas operators, EPC contractors, and service companies.
	Monitor and manage tender opportunities to ensure timely and compliant submissions.
	Provide technical support and product recommendations to clients based on operational requirements.
	Coordinate product delivery, logistics, and customer service activities to ensure client satisfaction.
	Track market trends, competitor activities, and emerging opportunities within the sector.
	Prepare sales forecasts, pipeline reports, and business development updates for management.


Requirements


	Bachelor's degree in Chemistry, Chemical Engineering, Industrial Chemistry, Petroleum Engineering, or a related discipline.
	Minimum of 3–5 years of proven sales experience in drilling, production, or completion chemicals within the Oil & Gas sector.
	Demonstrated success in independently generating revenue and achieving sales targets.
	Strong understanding of upstream oil and gas operations and chemical applications.
	Excellent knowledge of Oil & Gas procurement processes, tendering procedures, and vendor registration requirements.
	Strong technical sales and business development capabilities.
	Ability to prepare and present technical and commercial proposals.
	Strong negotiation, communication, and relationship management skills.
	Commercial awareness and ability to identify growth opportunities.
	Proficiency in Microsoft Office applications and CRM systems.


Salary
N200,000 - N400,000 per month.

Note: Only candidates resident in, or able to relocate to, Port Harcourt will be consider.
